memory: Executable provenance gates have a distinct continuity state from declarative contracts. A committed but unexecuted gate becomes runtime-verified only after the recorded source blobs are executed and the command, result, limits, and falsification route are retained.

observed: The committed validator and test blobs were retrieved.
Four deterministic Node tests passed with zero failures.

inferred: The validator contract is runtime-verified, while exhaustive repository traversal remains unverified.
proposed: Run an authenticated paginated history collector and feed its independently retrieved path set into the validator.

privacy_boundary: Public repository governance code and synthetic fixtures only.
falsification_route: Rerun the recorded command against the recorded blob identities in a clean authenticated checkout. Any mismatch or failure reopens the runtime-status claim. Exhaustive-coverage claims remain unresolved until an independent authenticated traversal passes the validator.
